title: rosa × dumetorum var. collina
intro: Rosa × dumetorum var. collina, also known as the wild rose, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which are obtained from its flowers, leaves, and hips. The health benefits of Rosa × dumetorum var. collina include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
